---
title: "Sustainable Agriculture and Planetary Boundaries: Exploring the Socioeconomic and Socio-ecological footprints of Mexico’s Agricultural Sector."
description: " "
date: "2026-06-12"
categories: [Article, LCA, Study Case, Agriculture, Mexico, SES, IIASA]
---

### Description

Conventional agriculture, heavily dependent on agrochemicals, provides affordable and widespread food around the world. Yet, it comes at a massive cost, driving climate change, biodiversity loss, environmental degradation, and impacts on human health. Transitioning to alternatives, including agroecological systems, isn't easy and straightforward; these systems face steep structural barriers, often requiring intense labour, specialised inputs (e.g., biofertilisants) and varied practices and capital assets.

To understand the real diversity and complexity of conventional and agroecological systems and their diversified agricultural practices, we processed a dataset covering more than 50,000 Mexican farms. We assembled farming archetypes based on observed practices, to which we coupled socioeconomic and socio-ecological footprints.

**OBS!** This work took place while attending the Young Scientists Summer Program at the [International Institute for Applied Systems Analysis (IIASA).](https://iiasa.ac.at/early-career/yssp) A Preliminary harmonised dataset is available.

* [Historical Agronomic Dataset - Insights from Mexico (2012 - 2022). Expanded and harmonized version. (early view, SQL)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17225594)
